factors.name: US Labor Market Weakness (NFP)
factors.summary: Thursday's softer-than-expected nonfarm payrolls data has reduced Fed rate-hike expectations, particularly for September, lowering near-term real yields. This is typically supportive for long-duration growth stocks like mega-cap tech.

factors.name: Fed Policy Repricing
factors.summary: Market now pricing ~50% odds of a November rate hike (vs higher expectations prior), and doubts on September hikes have deepened. Lower real yields benefit NDX-heavy tech names with long cash-flow durations.

factors.name: Macro Momentum & Risk-On
factors.summary: Global stocks hit historic all-time highs pre-holiday; Bitcoin at $62.3K; AUD, GBP, NZD, EUR all strengthening on USD weakness post-NFP. Risk appetite intact despite heat wave/power grid tensions.

factors.name: US Power Grid Stress & Heat Wave
factors.summary: Extreme heat threatening US power grids and forcing emergency curbs; could disrupt Q3 earnings/operations and cap upside momentum into late summer. Electricity supply risk may weigh on data center / AI capex near-term.

factors.name: Geopolitical Calm (Iran-US Deal)
factors.summary: OPEC output surge, Strait of Hormuz reopening, and US-Iran talks reducing energy shock fears. Lower oil (~$68.65 WTI) reduces inflation pressure and supports equities; gold weakness also aligns with lower real yields benefiting equities.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Window
factors.summary: No major FAANG earnings in news window; Q2 reporting season largely concluded. Next catalysts are earnings guidance/conference calls and capital allocation announcements (e.g., CPP's $1.75B EQT AI infrastructure deal signals continued capex appetite).

factors.name: AI & Semiconductor Sentiment
factors.summary: Modest AI bullishness (Kleiner Perkins VC outlook, robotics hype) but no major semiconductor supply or demand shocks; Zcash/crypto noise unrelated to NDX.

factors.name: USD Weakness Post-NFP
factors.summary: USD/JPY, USD/CHF, USD/CNH all weakening on softer labor data. Weaker dollar reduces headwinds for NQ international revenues and typically supports equity multiples.

factors.name: Existing Move (4h: +0.44%, 24h: -1.24%)
factors.summary: NAS100 flat to modestly positive in quiet US holiday trading (Independence Day). Bounce from -1.24% 24h low is underway but move is small and lacks conviction; holiday liquidity is thin.

factors.name: Real Yields (TIPS)
factors.summary: Softer NFP should push real yields lower (5Y TIPS expected to compress), directly bullish for long-duration growth; no competing inflation surprise to extend nominal yields sharply higher.

screening.narrative: NAS100 opened Q3 mixed after a blockbuster H1, with Thursday's weaker-than-expected NFP data triggering a dovish repricing of Fed policy (Sept hike odds now ~50% vs prior bullish bets). This has supported equities by pushing real yields lower, benefiting long-duration mega-cap tech. Geopolitical risks have eased (Iran-US deal, OPEC output surge) and global risk appetite remains intact at all-time highs. However, the move is unfolding in thin, holiday-shortened US trading, and a US power grid stress event from an extreme heat wave adds a near-term operational wildcard. The bias is cautiously bullish on fundamentals (lower real yields, dovish pivot) but tempered by phase-2 consolidation and lack of fresh mega-cap catalysts.
catalysts_ahead: Services PMI / ISM Non-Manufacturing PMI (Monday, 2026-07-07)
Fed Minutes (likely early–mid week, will clarify Sept hike intentions)
Weekly jobless claims (Thursday)
Mega-cap earnings guidance / capital allocation (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL guidance windows vary; check earnings calendar)
10Y Treasury auction (next week; will test real yield floor)
CPI / PCE inflation data (mid-month; biggest NDX driver if surprise occurs)
FOMC meeting (likely July 29–30; will reset rate expectations)
screening.risk_warning: If another hot inflation print (CPI, PCE) arrives mid-month, or if Fed speakers push back on dovish repricing, real yields could re-steepen and crush NDX multiples. Power grid failures or broader macro disruption from heat wave could trigger liquidity crisis / circuit breakers. Also: thin holiday liquidity can reverse moves sharply; watch for whipsaw into re-open (Tuesday).
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early phase-2 consolidation; avoid chasing up into resistance 29,450–29,600. Wait for retest of 29,100–29,150 support zone with bullish divergence (lower lows on volume) or break above 29,600 with conviction before committing. Holiday liquidity is thin—size down and expect slippage.

factors.name: NFP surprise (Thursday)
factors.summary: Softer-than-expected US jobs data released Thursday weakened USD and reduced Fed hike odds into September. This typically supports growth equities and multiples, but market has already priced initial relief.

factors.name: Real yields / nominal yield trajectory
factors.summary: No new Treasury data in news window. Gold rallied >1% on weaker NFP and is up $53 to $4,147, signaling lower real yields, but reaction appears 1 day old. Monitor 10Y yield for fresh directional clue.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings cycle status
factors.summary: No earnings surprises or guidance revisions reported in last 24h. Q2 earnings season is winding down; major drivers (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL) already reported. Forward catalysts unclear.

factors.name: AI capex and hyperscaler commentary
factors.summary: Mention of CPP investing $1.75B in EQT's AI infrastructure and Nvidia robotics thesis, but no new earnings calls or capex guidance in this window. Sentiment remains constructive but no fresh catalyst.

factors.name: Semiconductor supply chain
factors.summary: SK Hynix ADR offering and Chile lithium expansion reported, but no supply shock or demand cliff signal. No TSMC/ASML/Samsung production warnings.

factors.name: USD weakness
factors.summary: Dollar weakened sharply post-NFP; GBP +1% on week, AUD, NZD, EUR all rallied. Weaker USD typically bullish for NAS100 (international revenue tailwind), though move is 1 day old.

factors.name: VIX / risk sentiment
factors.summary: Global stocks hit all-time highs pre-holiday (Dow, market cap records noted). Bitcoin rallied to $62.3K nine-day high. Risk-on tone supportive, but muted by US Independence Day holiday (thin trading).

factors.name: China macro / geopolitics
factors.summary: China PMI resilient per Societe Generale; Yuan extending gains. Coast Guard Taiwan patrol rotation noted but no escalation. No chip export control tightening reported.

factors.name: Fed policy / rate expectations
factors.summary: Market pricing 50% odds of BOE hike in November and doubts Fed will hike in September post-NFP. Lower-for-longer narrative supports long-duration growth multiples if sustained.

factors.name: Holiday trading and volatility
factors.summary: US markets closed July 4; Asia/Europe trading thin. NFP relief rally from Thursday may reverse or consolidate on low volume. Avoid chasing in thin holiday sessions.

screening.narrative: NAS100 has bounced modestly (+0.44% in 4h) off Thursday's softer NFP print, which triggered a broad risk-on rebound and USD weakness. Global equities hit all-time highs; Bitcoin rallied to 9-day highs; gold broke $4,147. However, the move is 1+ days old and in thin holiday trading (US closed for Independence Day). Tech earnings cycle is winding down with no major guidance surprises in the news window, and hyperscaler capex commentary is stale. The bias remains constructively tilted on lower real yields and dovish Fed repricing, but conviction is muted by extended move and lack of fresh catalysts.
catalysts_ahead: US markets reopen post-July 4 — watch opening gaps and breadth into Tuesday
Fed minutes (timing TBD) — may clarify September rate hold conviction
CPI, PPI prints next week — key inflation test; any surprise hot could reverse rally
PCE personal spending data — household spending trends amid 'stressed consumer' narrative (General Mills warning)
FOMC meeting minutes release — transparency on labor-market and inflation debate
Mega-cap earnings revisions and guidance updates from Q2 season close-out
Treasury 10Y yield move — critical for tech multiple re-rating; watch 4.0–4.2% range
screening.risk_warning: If CPI or PCE data surprise hot next week, or if Fed minutes signal hawkish pivot (rejecting market's September hold pricing), the NFP-driven relief rally will reverse and real yields will spike, hitting long-duration Nasdaq names hard. Conversely, if 10Y yields break below 4.0%, upside could accelerate to 29,800+. Holiday thin volume makes stops unreliable; avoid aggressive leverage until US market reopens. China slowdown (PMI resilience but structural demand risks) or geopolitical flare (Taiwan, Middle East) could trigger risk-off repositioning.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is constructively tilted but move is extended (1+ day old in thin holiday session). On reopening, await retest of 29,150–29,250 support or a pullback from intraday highs before aggressive long entries. If CPI surprises, be prepared for sharp reversal to 28,900–29,000. Short entries only after failed break above 29,600 resistance; real-yield reacceleration or Fed hawkish signal would validate.

factors.name: US Labor Market Weakness (NFP)
factors.summary: Thursday's softer-than-expected NFP data triggered broad USD weakness and reduced Fed hike expectations. This typically supports long-duration growth assets like Nasdaq, as lower rate-hike odds compress real yields and boost tech multiples.

factors.name: Real Yields (TIPS) Trajectory
factors.summary: Weaker labor data and reduced hawkish Fed bets have cooled real yield pressure. Lower real yields are a major tailwind for high-duration Nasdaq constituents (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA).

factors.name: 10-Year Treasury Yield Momentum
factors.summary: Yields fell sharply Thursday on NFP miss, but Friday (US holiday, thin trading) saw stabilization. No fresh treasury data to confirm continued downside; expect repricing when US markets reopen in full force.

factors.name: Dollar Direction
factors.summary: USD weakened materially on softer NFP, supporting risk assets globally. Weaker dollar is structurally bullish for Nasdaq's international revenue streams, but move is already ~24h old (extended phase).

factors.name: AI / Tech Sentiment
factors.summary: No new mega-cap earnings, hyperscaler guidance, or AI capex announcements in the news window. Crypto correlation signals (BTC relief rally, AI infrastructure bets like CPP-EQT $1.75B investment) are supportive but non-critical.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Earnings Calendar
factors.summary: No imminent NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, or AMZN earnings announcements in the next 24-72h from the news provided. Catalyst void limits upside conviction.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran, Ukraine, Taiwan)
factors.summary: Iran funeral ceremonies, Ukraine drone attacks on Russian oil, and cross-strait military posturing are present but not acutely escalated. Oil stabilized around $68.65; no immediate shock risk to growth expectations.

factors.name: Antitrust / Regulatory Headwinds
factors.summary: EU MiCA enforcement, CBDC bans, and crypto regulation chatter are tangential to core Nasdaq drivers. No fresh DOJ/FTC actions against AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, or AMZN in this window.

factors.name: Price Action / Technical Positioning
factors.summary: NAS100 is +0.44% in 4h but -1.24% in 24h. The +0.44% 4h bounce is a partial recovery from Thursday's weakness, indicating a Phase 2 move (rebound underway, not confirmed). Late-cycle bounce risk exists if Friday strength fades into next week.

factors.name: VIX / Volatility Regime
factors.summary: No explicit VIX or VXN data in the news window, but thin US holiday trading and modest price swings suggest low volatility compression. Option flows and dealer gamma effects not visible from headlines.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq is in early-stage recovery mode following Thursday's NFP miss, which cooled Fed hike odds and supported a 0.44% 4h bounce. Real yields have eased, and the weaker USD is structurally bullish for Nasdaq's large overseas revenue base. However, the broader 24h chart shows -1.24% weakness, and no new mega-cap earnings, AI capex announcements, or hyperscaler guidance is imminent. Friday's US Independence Day holiday meant thin trading and subdued conviction; next week's full reopening and any macro surprises (FOMC minutes, PCE inflation, upcoming earnings) will be critical to sustaining or reversing this bounce.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Meeting Minutes (week of July 8) – will clarify Fed's true stance on rate cuts and real-yield trajectory
PCE / Core PCE inflation print – critical for growth multiple re-pricing
Next NFP release – labor market trajectory remains central to policy path
Mega-cap earnings season ramp (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, AMZN) – exact dates TBD, but typically mid/late July
10-Year Treasury yield stability vs. new lows – real rates will determine if Nasdaq rally sustains
Jobless claims data – ongoing labor market deterioration would extend dovish repricing
screening.risk_warning: If NFP surprise reverses next week (stronger-than-expected employment) or inflation (PCE) comes hot, real yields will spike and reverse the bullish setup. Thin Friday trading may not reflect Monday-Tuesday institutional repositioning. Nasdaq also faces extended downside if Trump tariff/policy uncertainty (CUSMA non-renewal, China tensions, crypto regulation) suddenly dominate. A failed retest of 29,200 support could trigger a fresh leg down to 28,800–29,000.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already in Phase 2 (rebound 24h old). Await retest of 29,100–29,200 support on next dip before considering long entries; confirmation above 29,600 resistance needed to justify conviction 7+. Avoid chasing on thin holiday liquidity; wait for Monday full market open.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations
factors.summary: Softer-than-expected US NFP data (released Thursday) has trimmed hawkish rate hike bets; market now pricing ~50% odds of November BOE hike and reduced September Fed hike conviction. Lower rate expectations support long-duration growth multiples.

factors.name: US Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD has weakened following weak labor data; GBP and other currencies posting 1%+ weekly gains. Weaker USD typically supports NDX components with international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Real Yields (TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS data in news window; Treasury yields are stabilizing after Thursday's decline. Real yield direction remains unclear without concrete inflation data or Fed guidance.

factors.name: Inflation Data & PCE
factors.summary: No new CPI, Core CPI, or PCE prints in the 24h news window. ECB's Moulin noted inflation has eased with oil price slump, but no US inflation surprises reported to confirm deflationary trend.

factors.name: Mega-cap Tech Earnings
factors.summary: No major tech earnings announcements or guidance in news cycle. Earnings season is not imminent; next high-impact earnings likely weeks away.

factors.name: AI/Hyperscaler Capex
factors.summary: CPP Investment Board announced $1.75B investment in EQT's AI infrastructure buildout; broader macro narrative emphasizes AI data center investment across France, India, and Asia. Supports secular NDX tailwind.

factors.name: Semiconductor Supply Chain
factors.summary: SK Hynix considering 0.5% fee on blockbuster US share sale; SQM-Codelco planning 70%+ lithium output boost. Supply-side momentum constructive but no major TSMC, ASML, or NVDA-specific supply disruptions reported.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Middle East)
factors.summary: Tanker U-turn in Hormuz, Iran funeral ceremonies, Russia oil terminal attacked, but oil prices falling (~$68.65 WTI) on reopening optimism. Reduced near-term risk premium; longer-term Middle East tension persists but priced in.

factors.name: China-Taiwan, Chip Export Controls
factors.summary: China Coast Guard rotated patrol task group east of Taiwan; routine operational posture, not escalatory. No new export control announcements from US or allies.

factors.name: Market Sentiment & Crypto Correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin rebounding off 43-month low profit/loss ratio, XRP up 8%, SOL rallies on memecoin activity. Risk-on sentiment improving; NDX-BTC correlation typically positive in dip-buying phases.

factors.name: VIX / Tech Vol (VXN)
factors.summary: No VIX or VXN data in news feed. Historical context: VXN likely remains elevated following Thursday's NFP miss, but tech vol typically normalizes into long weekends.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD stabilizing but weakening post-NFP; EUR/USD holding modest gains, AUD/USD rising near 0.6940, GBP posting 1%+ weekly gains. Broad dollar weakness is structurally supportive for US-listed tech with global revenue.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is treading water after Thursday's softer-than-expected NFP spooked growth markets but subsequently triggered a dovish repricing of Fed rate expectations. Friday's weak 4h move (+0.44%) and negative 24h change (-1.24%) suggest the initial shock has been absorbed, but no decisive directional conviction has emerged. Risk sentiment is shifting back toward 'risk-on' (Bitcoin, SOL, XRP rallying; AI capex momentum intact), yet lack of fresh macro catalysts (no CPI, no Fed speakers) and a holiday-shortened US trading week leaves traders range-bound. The dominant narrative remains 'Fed pause = lower real yields = bullish for duration/growth,' but execution is muted.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Minutes release (upcoming week): clarity on September rate path
Jobless Claims (weekly): confirmation of labor market softness or rebound
Earnings season preview: tech guidance updates starting mid-July
ECB Ecofin meeting (next week): Lagarde attendance, policy signaling
CPI/PCE inflation data: next major inflation print will re-price real yields
10-year Treasury yield action: any 50bp rally would compress growth multiples
screening.risk_warning: If CPI or PCE surprise HOT (>3.5% YoY), real yields spike, and the dovish repricing reverses, NDX will re-test support aggressively. Conversely, escalation in Middle East (Strait of Hormuz closure, oil spike) could trigger safe-haven flows but would likely pressure growth multiples. Upcoming antitrust/regulatory scrutiny (EU DMA, DOJ) could rattle mega-cap individual names. Holiday liquidity and thin positioning make intraday reversals probable.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is nascent and price action is choppy; await a clear close above 29,500 resistance or test of support near 29,100 with volume confirmation before committing. Friday's framing as Phase 1 refresh is fragile given US holiday and thin flows; wait for Monday/Tuesday data or Fed-speak for higher-conviction entry.

factors.name: NFP Miss (July 3)
factors.summary: Softer-than-expected US nonfarm payrolls released Thursday cooled Fed hike expectations, reducing rate-hike odds and supporting long-duration growth equities. FXStreet and Bloomberg both highlighted weaker labor data as USD-negative and equity-positive on Friday.

factors.name: Oil Crash on Iran Peace Deal
factors.summary: Bloomberg reported oil prices 'tumbling everywhere' as US-Iran peace deal unleashes supply wave, overwhelming demand and fueling glut concerns. Lower energy costs are mildly disinflationary but do not directly support mega-cap tech valuations.

factors.name: Real Yields & Fed Policy Path
factors.summary: NFP miss suggests Fed is likely pausing; market now pricing ~50% odds of Nov hike vs prior hawkish bias. ECB's Moulin said ECB in 'good position' post-hike, but no major US rate catalyst imminent until FOMC minutes or next CPI. Real yields remain structurally elevated, limiting multiple expansion.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Earnings Calendar
factors.summary: No specific NAS100 earnings mentioned in last 24h news window. Q2 season will begin mid-July; currently in quiet period. Lack of near-term catalysts limits conviction.

factors.name: Semiconductor & AI Capex Commentary
factors.summary: CPP investing $1.75B in EQT AI infrastructure (positive signal), but no major hyperscaler capex guidance or NVDA/TSMC supply updates in this cycle. SK Hynix considering ADR underwriting fees (neutral corporate activity).

factors.name: Tech Sentiment & Risk-On Positioning
factors.summary: Bitcoin bounced off multi-year lows with spot BTC ETF inflow of $221M on July 2, and global stock market cap hit all-time highs per CoinDesk. NAS100 often correlates with risk appetite and crypto rebounds on softer macro.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD stabilized but remains under pressure post-NFP; weaker dollar supports international revenue exposure typical of Nasdaq mega-caps (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL). FXStreet noted broad USD weakness on weak jobless claims.

factors.name: Macro Chop & Holiday Liquidity
factors.summary: US markets closed July 4 (Independence Day); thin trading volumes on Friday. Post-holiday chop and rebalancing flows could add volatility. No major catalyst in next 24-48h raises whipsaw risk.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tail Risks
factors.summary: Iran funeral, Ukraine attacks, China-Taiwan patrols mentioned but already priced into oil/commodity markets. No new escalation signals; unlikely to drive near-term NAS100 repricing.

factors.name: Price Action Context
factors.summary: 4h change +0.44%, 24h change -1.24%. Modest intraday bounce on Friday but still down 1.24% over 24h, indicating sellers retain control. Move not yet extended enough to suggest reversal.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is stuck in post-NFP relief bounce mode after Thursday's weaker-than-expected jobs data triggered Fed-pause expectations and modest USD weakness. Global risk appetite improved (BTC rallied, stock market cap hit ATH), supporting long-duration growth assets. However, liquidity is thin on Friday due to July 4 US holiday closure, and real yields remain structurally elevated, capping multiple expansion. Oil's sharp collapse on Iran peace deal is disinflationary but does not directly benefit tech valuations. No major earnings catalyst or Fed commentary in next 48–72h, leaving the market vulnerable to chop and mean reversion.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Minutes (likely early July, timing TBD) — could clarify Sept/Nov rate path
CPI/PCE data (mid-July) — next major inflation test to validate soft-landing narrative
NVIDIA, Microsoft, Google earnings (mid-July) — critical for AI capex guidance and hyperscaler demand signals
Fed Fund Futures repricing on any commentary — markets still digesting June NFP surprise
Treasury 10Y yield direction — key monitor for real-rate impact on growth multiples
screening.risk_warning: If Friday's bounce is a false start and equity indices re-test 24h lows on Monday reopen, bias flips BEARISH. Conversely, if BTC sustains bounce above $65K and ETF inflows accelerate into Monday, risk-on tone could break NAS100 above 29,500. FOMC minutes or surprise hawkish Fed commentary would crush the move. Real yields creeping higher (10Y > 4.1%) would be the most damaging factor for Nasdaq multiples.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (relief bounce underway but not yet extended); wait for either a retest of 29,100–29,200 support with higher volume on Monday reopen, or a break above 29,500 resistance with confirmed QQQ/SMH follow-through before considering new exposure. Current levels are range-chop territory — avoid top-ticking bounces in thin holiday liquidity.

factors.name: US Labor Market Weakness (NFP)
factors.summary: Softer-than-expected US Nonfarm Payrolls data (mentioned across FXStreet, MarketWatch) weighed on USD and reduced Fed rate hike expectations. Weaker labor data typically supports equity multiples by pushing out rate-hike timeline.

factors.name: Real Yields / Fed Repricing
factors.summary: Market pricing suggests ~50% odds of November BOE hike (per Investinglive), and Fed hike bets have weakened. Lower real yields should support long-duration Nasdaq tech stocks, though move is still early.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse & Geopolitical De-escalation
factors.summary: Iran peace deal unleashed supply wave, oil tumbling (Bloomberg: 'Oil's Supply Wave, Tumbling Prices Rekindle Fears of Global Glut'). Lower energy costs could boost consumer purchasing power, but energy sector weakness and inflationary narrative shift creates mixed sentiment for growth stocks.

factors.name: Crypto Weakness / Risk-Off Sentiment
factors.summary: Bitcoin at 43-month profit/loss lows, extended weakness despite recent relief rally (CoinDesk: 'Bitcoin's next parabolic run may need $1 trillion in fresh capital'). Risk-off crypto sentiment typically correlates with Nasdaq caution, though AI/hyperscaler narrative remains intact.

factors.name: AI Capex & Hyperscaler Momentum
factors.summary: Multiple sources (Decrypt, Bloomberg, MarketWatch) confirm sustained AI infrastructure investment and government courtship of AI giants (Macron, Modi). Nvidia robotics opportunity and continued cloud capex guidance remain structural supports for mega-cap tech.

factors.name: Holiday Shortened Week / Thin Liquidity
factors.summary: US markets closed July 4 (Independence Day), reducing trading volume and conviction. Thin liquidity often leads to chop and false breakouts; directional conviction is lower in holiday weeks.

factors.name: Treasury Yields / Duration
factors.summary: No major 10-year yield shock reported in recent 24h news; market reassessing Fed path post-weak NFP. Yield moves likely modest given holiday calendar and no FOMC statement imminent this week.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Cycle
factors.summary: No earnings surprises or guidance cuts mentioned in this news cycle. Q2 earnings season is winding down; next major catalyst would be Q3 earnings in Oct-Nov.

factors.name: VIX / Market Volatility
factors.summary: No mention of VIX spikes or options positioning shifts; market remains calm post-holiday. Volatility likely subdued until major macro or earnings catalyst emerges.

factors.name: China / Taiwan / Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: China coast guard rotation east of Taiwan (routine patrol). No escalation or chip export control news; geopolitical tail risk appears stable rather than a near-term driver.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between two conflicting narratives: (1) dovish Fed repricing from weak NFP data and collapsing oil prices (bullish for duration and equity multiples), versus (2) crypto/risk-asset weakness and extended weakness in breadth metrics (bearish sentiment). Holiday-shortened week with thin liquidity is suppressing conviction in either direction. AI capex and hyperscaler fundamentals remain constructive, but lack fresh catalysts. The index has been oscillating NEUTRAL (5/10) for three consecutive reads, suggesting a consolidation phase rather than a breakout setup.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Chair Powell commentary (if any scheduled)
Initial Jobless Claims (weekly, next Thurs Jul 10)
CPI / Core CPI print (likely mid-July)
FOMC meeting minutes or next policy decision (TBD)
Mega-cap tech earnings guidance updates (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA in pipeline for later summer)
Treasury auction results and yield curve repricing
Semiconductor/hyperscaler capex announcements or cloud guidance updates
screening.risk_warning: A sharp reversal in US dollar strength, hawkish Fed pivot, or major crypto capitulation could flip bias to BEARISH. Conversely, a strong rebound in labor data, dovish Fed surprise, or mega-cap tech earnings beat could spark rally. Holiday calendar and thin liquidity mean whipsaws are elevated; await return to normal trading volume before chasing breakouts.
screening.entry_guidance: Market in consolidation; no clear directional setup. Wait for either a decisive break above 29,550 (with volume confirmation post-holiday) or a retest of 29,100 support before committing. Consider only small positions until conviction builds with fresh macro data or earnings catalyst.

factors.name: Tech Earnings Pipeline Clarity

factors.summary: Multiple sources (Bloomberg, FXStreet) cite strong AI adoption (China PMI strength, Ray Dalio philanthropy piece, government AI center investments). Structural demand for Nvidia/cloud infrastructure remains intact.

factors.name: Macro Holiday & Thin Liquidity
factors.summary: US Independence Day (July 4, 2026) results in thin holiday trading and reduced institutional participation. Chop and low conviction moves typical. FXStreet noted 'thin trading' and holiday effects.

factors.name: 24h Price Action: -1.24% Decline
factors.summary: Nasdaq down 1.24% over 24h despite modest 4h recovery (+0.44%). Suggests morning weakness and failed rebound—potential distribution/weakness on lower participation.

key_levels.support: 29,100–29,200 (near-term 24h lows implied by -1.24% move from higher close)
key_levels.resistance: 29,500–29,600 (session highs, confluence with recent range tops)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq has treaded water post-holiday with a slight overnight decline (-1.24% 24h) despite softer NFP data that should have supported duration assets. The modest 4h recovery (+0.44%) suggests buyers are testing but haven't committed with conviction. Real yields remain the linchpin—lower fed expectations help NDX valuations, but thin holiday liquidity and lack of fresh mega-cap earnings catalysts are limiting fuel. AI structural story (China PMI strength, capex narratives) persists but isn't driving new highs.
catalysts_ahead: Fed meeting minutes (likely early-mid week)
Initial jobless claims (weekly, Thursday typical)
Mega-cap earnings season ramp (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL guidance critical for NDX direction)
Inflation data (PCE, CPI if scheduled within next 72h)
China economic data (manufacturing PMI trend)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Real yields spike >25bps on hot inflation surprise or hawkish Fed pivot, compressing growth multiples; (2) Major mega-cap earnings miss on capex guidance, rattling AI narrative; (3) USD rebounds sharply (China weakness, risk-off rotation); (4) Liquidity dries up further into July 4 close, creating vacuum-driven selloff. Conversely, confirmation requires sustained close >29,500 on volume post-holiday.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid aggressive entries into thin holiday liquidity. If dip buyers establish >29,400 on volume and 4h close above 29,350, consider small longs targeting 29,550. Otherwise, wait for post-holiday capitulation (~July 7–8) and fresh earnings catalysts before committing size.

factors.name: Macro data - Jobs & inflation
factors.summary: MarketWatch reported 'June's jobs and inflation data are bullish for bonds' with commentary that 'new jobs report is worse than many people realize,' suggesting labor market weakness that could ease Fed pressure but also signals economic slowdown headwinds for growth equities.

factors.name: Oil supply glut fears
factors.summary: Bloomberg reported 'Oil's Supply Wave, Tumbling Prices Rekindle Fears of Global Glut' following US-Iran peace deal unleashing supply. Lower energy prices reduce inflation but signal demand concerns and potential deflationary pressure.

factors.name: Fed policy expectations
factors.summary: ECB's Moulin stated ECB is in 'good position' post-rate hike with easing inflation; no fresh US Fed signals in news window. Market awaiting Fed minutes and jobless claims data next week per FXStreet.

factors.name: Real yields & duration sensitivity
factors.summary: No material move in 10Y Treasury or TIPS in news window. Tech mega-caps remain sensitive to real rate direction; lack of clarity on Fed pivot timing keeps duration longs uncertain.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings cycle
factors.summary: No earnings releases or forward guidance from AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA, TSLA in news window. Market in quiet post-4th of July period; next earnings cycle is forward-looking catalyst.

factors.name: AI capex & hyperscaler commentary
factors.summary: No new cloud capex guidance from AWS, Azure, GCP. Macro focus on AI regulation (EU restriction on retail crypto prediction markets, Anthropic AI safety debate) rather than growth driver commentary.

factors.name: Antitrust & regulatory risk
factors.summary: DOJ Adani case dismissed but broader EU DMA scrutiny of tech persists. No new mega-cap antitrust actions in this window, but regulatory headwinds remain a structural overhang for NDX.

factors.name: Semiconductor supply chain
factors.summary: Continental sold ContiTech to Lone Star; no TSMC, ASML, or Samsung news impacting chip supply or demand outlook. Semiconductor cycle outlook remains data-dependent on hyperscaler orders.

factors.name: VIX / Options flow
factors.summary: No VIX, VXN, or dealer gamma data in news window. 4h change +0.44% and 24h change -1.24% suggests minimal volatility; low momentum either direction.

factors.name: Crypto correlation (BTC/ETH)
factors.summary: Bitcoin jumped above $63k reversing end-June losses; ETH and SOL rallies noted. Risk-on crypto momentum could signal marginal improvement in tech risk appetite, though correlation to NDX remains loose.

factors.name: Dollar direction (DXY)
factors.summary: FXStreet noted 'US Dollar's resilience faces Fed minutes and jobless claims' next week. No directional DXY move in window; weak jobs data typically weakens USD and supports NDX valuations.

factors.name: Price action context
factors.summary: 4h change +0.44% (minimal), 24h change -1.24% (modest pullback). No clear directional momentum; range-bound consolidation consistent with NEUTRAL bias from prior three reads.

screening.narrative: NAS100 remains in consolidation post-4th of July holiday with soft labor data and oil supply glut fears offsetting marginal crypto-driven risk-on sentiment. Weak jobs report and easing oil prices suggest potential dovish Fed repricing, which would be positive for duration-sensitive growth tech, but economic slowdown signals create offsetting headwind. Market is in a wait-and-see posture ahead of Fed minutes and jobless claims (likely Thursday–Friday this week), with no fresh mega-cap earnings or hyperscaler capex guidance to drive immediate conviction. Bias has held NEUTRAL across three consecutive reads.
catalysts_ahead: Fed minutes release (likely mid-week this week)
Weekly jobless claims data (Thursday)
FOMC-linked repricing on inflation/growth expectations
Mega-cap earnings season ramp (TBA specific dates)
Core PCE / CPI print if scheduled this week
screening.risk_warning: A hawkish Fed surprise (minutes hint at higher-for-longer rates or sticky inflation) would break the dovish bias and compress tech multiples sharply; conversely, weak jobless claims could trigger 'Fed pivot' rally pushing NQ higher. Oil rebound on geopolitical risk would support energy and undercut deflation narrative. Watch for any mega-cap preannouncements or guidance revisions that could reignite single-name volatility.
screening.entry_guidance: Market remains rangebound and unfocused; avoid chasing either direction until Fed minutes or macro data clarifies real-yield trajectory. Consider entry on pullback to 29,100 support (if staying NEUTRAL) or on break above 29,700 resistance (if dovish data triggers risk-on). Otherwise defer until post-FOMC clarity.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings cycle
factors.summary: No earnings releases or guidance in the 24h news window. Next cycle catalyst timing unknown, but this remains the primary driver of NQ directional moves.

factors.name: Fed policy and rate expectations
factors.summary: No FOMC decision, rate guidance, or Fed speaker commentary in recent news. Macro backdrop remains uncertain pending next inflation and employment data.

factors.name: Inflation data (CPI/PCE/NFP)
factors.summary: No fresh CPI, PCE, or NFP print in the 24h window. MarketWatch mentions 'June's jobs and inflation data are bullish for bonds' but no specific figures provided; unable to assess surprise direction.

factors.name: Treasury yield (10Y)
factors.summary: No 10Y yield data or Treasury news in the 24h window. Long-duration growth multiple sensitivity to real yields remains a primary structural risk.

factors.name: Dollar strength (DXY/USD)
factors.summary: Goldman Sachs revised USD/JPY forecasts and stated 'no return to broad-based dollar weakness anytime soon,' suggesting structural USD strength, but broader DXY data absent. Tech heavy international revenue exposure makes this material.

factors.name: AI/semiconductor capex sentiment
factors.summary: Tokenization, Kraken collateral moves, and prediction market traction mentioned (Kalshi record June volume), but no hyperscaler cloud capex guidance, NVDA commentary, or chip order updates in the 24h feed.

factors.name: Crypto correlation (NQ-BTC linkage)
factors.summary: Bitcoin jumped above $63,000 reversing end-June losses; BTC spot ETF inflows of $221M on July 2 noted. Risk-on sentiment in crypto may support NQ, though crypto rally has not yet driven broad NDX follow-through (24h change -1.24%).

factors.name: Geopolitical / macro risks
factors.summary: Iran funeral, Venezuela earthquake death toll (2,954), Ukraine-Russia war commentary, and domestic US political divisions (Patriot Front march, VP Vance UK criticism) create headline noise and potential tail-risk tail hedging, though no direct market impact reported yet.

factors.name: VIX / volatility regime
factors.summary: No explicit VIX or VXN print provided. Heat wave and thunderstorms disrupting July 4 events may inject near-term micro disruptions but lack macro volatility significance.

factors.name: EU growth / earnings season preview
factors.summary: Bloomberg notes German data 'in coming week' will reflect Iran war impact; European earnings season preview flagged. No surprise direction yet; watch for potential NDX drag if eurozone weakness emerges.

factors.name: Antitrust / regulatory risk
factors.summary: No DOJ, FTC, or DMA enforcement actions against mega-cap tech in 24h window. EU crypto regulation and prediction market restrictions mentioned but not directly material to NQ mega-caps.

screening.narrative: NAS100 remains locked in a corrective consolidation following a -1.24% 24h decline, with only modest +0.44% 4h recovery. No fresh macro catalyst or earnings news emerged in the 24h window to break the stalemate. Bitcoin's pop above $63k suggests risk-on micro-sentiment (crypto ETF inflows), but this has NOT yet translating into sustained NQ upside; the index is still net negative on the day. Geopolitical static (Iran funeral, Venezuela quake, Ukraine) and Goldman's 'no near-term dollar weakness' commentary create mild headwinds for long-duration growth, while the absence of any Fed/CPI/earnings catalyst means the market is treading water ahead of likely US macro data drops (NFP, CPI) in coming days.
catalysts_ahead: US jobs report (NFP) — likely early July, will reset Fed rate expectations and long-duration tech multiple outlook
US inflation data (CPI/Core CPI/PCE) — likely early-mid July, critical for fed funds futures and 10Y Treasury repricing
Mega-cap tech Q2 earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA, TSLA) — late July/early Aug, likely to dominate NQ moves 2-3% per guidance surprise
FOMC decision and Fed guidance — late July, critical for nominal and real yield reset
European earnings season data release (early-mid July) — could reveal eurozone weakness under geopolitical strain
screening.risk_warning: Lack of fresh macro/earnings catalyst creates false-consolidation risk; if a surprise hot CPI or hawkish Fed commentary drops in next 24-48h, rapid 10Y yield spike could trigger 2-3% selloff in long-duration tech (NQ) before bounce. Conversely, if NFP/CPI comes in cooler than expected, risk-on rally could rapidly break above 29,600 resistance. Geopolitical flare (Iran, Taiwan, Russia) remains a tail-hedging concern but has not yet materialized into market disruption.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 consolidation; avoid chasing. Wait for either (a) break above 29,600 on fresh bullish macro catalyst (dovish Fed, cool CPI) with volume confirmation, or (b) flush below 29,200 support into a retest of prior lows — both offer better risk/reward than current sideways range. Monitor overnight crypto and Treasuries for early signals.
